task: I’m thinking about signing up for a couple of online research-study platforms, but before I hand over my info I want a practical sense of what the participant experience is actually like. Please start on Google and look up Respondent.io, then open the actual Respondent site and any clearly relevant public help or participant pages so you can tell me, in plain English, how it works for participants — especially how someone signs up, builds a profile, finds or qualifies for studies, and how payment is handled. Keep the most useful Respondent page open so I can glance at it later. Then go to Terac’s site and figure out how that platform works for participants too, with extra attention to how someone gets set up to take part in studies, because I want to compare whether Terac’s onboarding feels simpler or more involved than Respondent’s. If you find a page that explains joining or participation, leave that open in its own tab as proof. After that, switch over to SurveyMonkey and open the screener page and verify it loads with visible survey questions so I can see what an actual participant flow feels like end to end, and leave that final page visible. When you’re done, give me a short comparison of Respondent versus Terac, say clearly whether Terac seems easier or more involved to get started with, and confirm that the SurveyMonkey screener page loaded with visible survey questions.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully visited the Respondent.io website and relevant help pages (e.g., "How Respondent works (and how to earn money)"). In its final response, the agent provided a clear summary of the participant experience, covering:
1. Signup/profile creation (creating a free profile).
2. Finding/qualifying for studies (matching algorithm based on background/interests, answering screening questions).
3. Payment handling (virtual Visa cards via Tremendous).
The agent also kept the relevant Respondent help page open as requested. All elements of the rubric are satisfied.

R2: success - The agent successfully visited the Terac website (https://terac.com/) and explored its participant-facing features. Through the action history, the agent interacted with the site, expanded FAQ sections (e.g., "What is the AI interview?", "How much do I get paid?", "What qualifications do I need?"), and summarized the platform's purpose (expert-focused research) and the participant setup flow (claiming a profile, signing in via third-party accounts, and completing an AI assessment/interview). The agent also left the Terac site open as a proof tab. The summary provided in the final response accurately reflects the information gathered from the site.

R3: success - The agent provided a clear comparison between Respondent and Terac. It explicitly stated that Respondent is easier to get started with and that Terac is more involved, citing the expert verification and AI interview/onboarding process as the reasons for this difference. This directly satisfies the requirement of the rubric.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to a SurveyMonkey screener page (https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/6F6Z8M3) as evidenced by the snapshot in step 30 and 31. The snapshot clearly shows the presence of visible survey questions, including text boxes for "Name" and "Country," as well as radio buttons for participation consent. The agent left this page open as the final visible tab, satisfying the requirement to verify the page loads with visible questions and leave it as proof.

rubric_results.requirement: Summarize how Respondent.io works for participants, including signup/profile creation, how users find or qualify for studies, and how payment is handled, based on publicly available information from official or clearly relevant pages.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms the final response includes all four elements and that a relevant Respondent page is open or was visited from Google search results.
rubric_results.final_reasoning:
The agent successfully visited the Respondent.io website and relevant help pages (e.g., "How Respondent works (and how to earn money)"). In its final response, the agent provided a clear summary of the participant experience, covering:
1. Signup/profile creation (creating a free profile).
2. Finding/qualifying for studies (matching algorithm based on background/interests, answering screening questions).
3. Payment handling (virtual Visa cards via Tremendous).
The agent also kept the relevant Respondent help page open as requested. All elements of the rubric are satisfied.

rubric_results.requirement: Summarize how Terac works and specifically explain how participants get set up to take part in studies using information from Terac’s public site.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms the final response describes Terac’s platform purpose and participant setup flow, and that a relevant Terac page explaining participation or joining is open or was visited.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully visited the Terac website (https://terac.com/) and explored its participant-facing features. Through the action history, the agent interacted with the site, expanded FAQ sections (e.g., "What is the AI interview?", "How much do I get paid?", "What qualifications do I need?"), and summarized the platform's purpose (expert-focused research) and the participant setup flow (claiming a profile, signing in via third-party accounts, and completing an AI assessment/interview). The agent also left the Terac site open as a proof tab. The summary provided in the final response accurately reflects the information gathered from the site.

rubric_results.requirement: Directly compare Terac’s onboarding/setup with Respondent’s baseline and state clearly whether Terac appears easier or more involved for participants.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms the final response contains an explicit comparison and a clear easier/more involved judgment grounded in the two platform summaries.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided a clear comparison between Respondent and Terac. It explicitly stated that Respondent is easier to get started with and that Terac is more involved, citing the expert verification and AI interview/onboarding process as the reasons for this difference. This directly satisfies the requirement of the rubric.

rubric_results.requirement: Open the SurveyMonkey screener page and verify it loads with visible survey questions, leaving the page visible as proof.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the SurveyMonkey screener page was opened and that visible survey questions or screener content are displayed on the page.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully navigated to a SurveyMonkey screener page (https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/6F6Z8M3) as evidenced by the snapshot in step 30 and 31. The snapshot clearly shows the presence of visible survey questions, including text boxes for "Name" and "Country," as well as radio buttons for participation consent. The agent left this page open as the final visible tab, satisfying the requirement to verify the page loads with visible questions and leave it as proof.
